backlog.title: Voice-Typing Bug Fix: Toggle Error Surfacing, Spurious Drain, and Config Validation
backlog.description: Fix the three issues identified by adversarial end-to-end validation: (1) cross-mode toggle failure silently returns ok:true instead of surfacing the model-load error over the control socket; (2) stale _final_pending from late realtime partials causes a spurious 5-second drain on stop when nothing is in flight; (3) unknown output.backend value is not validated at config load time. All three are confirmed with executable reproductions; the 421-test suite passes without these fixes. The three issues are INDEPENDENT (different files/methods, no execution-order dependency) and may be implemented in parallel. All changes are correctness/UX fixes — no data-loss or availability-breaking bugs. Each subtask follows implicit TDD: write the failing test first, implement, pass.

milestones.title: Issue 1: Surface cross-mode toggle load failures as ok:false in the control-socket response
milestones.description: When a toggle/toggle-lite command triggers a mode-switch model load that FAILS (CUDA OOM, missing model, cuDNN error), the ControlServer._dispatch() response must return {"ok": false, "error": "model load failed: ..."} so voicectl prints the error and exits 1 — matching what start/start-lite already do via _arm_response(). Currently the cross-mode failure returns {"ok": true, "listening": false} with zero indication of the failure. The daemon-level toggle()/toggle_lite() behavior is correct (they clear stale _listening and set _load_error); the bug is purely in the dispatch-layer response shaping.

tasks.title: Fix _dispatch() toggle/toggle-lite cross-mode failure response routing
tasks.description: Modify ControlServer._dispatch() so that when a toggle or toggle-lite command results in a failed cross-mode model load (was_listening=True but the target-mode _load_host() failed and disarmed), the response is routed through _arm_response() which returns ok:false + error. The existing arm_attempted flag computation misses this case because after the failed cross-mode switch is_listening() is False. The fix detects a freshly-set _load_error (snapshotted before vs after the toggle call) and routes through _arm_response() in that case.

subtasks.title: Add load-error-aware response routing for toggle/toggle-lite cross-mode failures in _dispatch()
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/006_862ee9d6ef41/bugfix/001_ce96ba984c11/architecture/system_context.md §Issue 1 and _scout_test_patterns.md §Issue 1. The bug is in ControlServer._dispatch() (voice_typing/daemon.py lines ~1918-1951), NOT in the daemon's toggle()/toggle_lite() methods (those are correct — tested by test_toggle_while_armed_in_lite_failed_reload_clears_listening at test_daemon.py:3877). The arm_attempted flag (line ~1935) is computed as `not was_listening or self._daemon.is_listening()`. For a cross-mode toggle failure: was_listening=True, toggle() fails+disarms, is_listening()=False, so arm_attempted=False, so the response falls through to `{'ok': True, **status_snapshot()}` instead of _arm_response(). _arm_response() (line ~1901) IS correct: it returns ok:false when _load_error is set AND not is_listening(). The _load_error lifecycle: _load_host() resets it to None at the start of every attempt (line 726), so snapshotting before/after the toggle() call reliably detects a FRESH failure. The client-side ctl.py format_result() needs NO change — it already renders ok:false as 'error: ...' with exit code 1.
2. INPUT: No upstream subtask dependency. The existing _arm_response() method (daemon.py:1901-1916) and _load_error attribute (daemon.py:661) are already in place and correct. The _failing_second_spawn_factory (test_daemon.py:3843-3856) and _FakeHost (test_daemon.py:532-616) test infrastructure already exist for simulating cross-mode reload failures.
3. LOGIC:
   A) TEST FIRST (failing test, RED): Add TWO tests to tests/test_daemon.py (near the existing cross-mode failure tests at line ~3834, or to tests/test_control_socket.py if it can import the failing-spawn factory). Each test constructs a REAL daemon via _make_lazy_daemon(host_factory=_failing_second_spawn_factory(spawns)), arms in one mode, wraps it in daemon.ControlServer(d), dispatches the cross-mode toggle, and asserts the response is {'ok': False} with 'model load failed' in the error string:
      - Test A: arm in lite (d.start_lite()), dispatch _dispatch(json.dumps({'cmd': 'toggle'})) → cross-mode switch lite→normal FAILS → assert resp['ok'] is False and 'model load failed' in resp['error'].
      - Test B: arm in normal (d.start()), dispatch _dispatch(json.dumps({'cmd': 'toggle-lite'})) → cross-mode switch normal→lite FAILS → assert resp['ok'] is False and 'model load failed' in resp['error'].
      These tests MUST FAIL (RED) before the fix because _dispatch currently returns {'ok': True, ...} for this path. Note: _make_lazy_daemon is in test_daemon.py and returns (d, fb); _failing_second_spawn_factory takes a `spawns` list. The first spawn (start_lite/start) succeeds, the second spawn (cross-mode toggle) fails. This is the EXISTING pattern from test_toggle_while_armed_in_lite_failed_reload_clears_listening.
   B) IMPLEMENT (GREEN): In ControlServer._dispatch(), modify BOTH the 'toggle' and 'toggle-lite' branches (daemon.py ~1931-1940 and ~1946-1952). Before calling self._daemon.toggle()/toggle_lite(), snapshot load_error_before = getattr(self._daemon, '_load_error', None). After the toggle call, add a check: if arm_attempted is False BUT load_error_before is None and getattr(self._daemon, '_load_error', None) is truthy (a fresh load error was set during this toggle), route through return self._arm_response() instead of return {'ok': True, **status_snapshot()}. The _arm_response() method already does the right thing (returns ok:false+error when _load_error set and not listening). DO NOT modify _arm_response(), toggle(), toggle_lite(), _load_host(), or ctl.py — they are all correct. The fix is ONLY in the two dispatch branches.
   C) VERIFY: Run `timeout 120 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_daemon.py -q -k 'cross_mode_toggle or toggle_failed or toggle_while_armed'` and `timeout 120 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_control_socket.py -q` and `timeout 120 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_voicectl.py -q`. All must pass. Also verify the two new tests pass.
   Mocking: No external services need mocking — _failing_second_spawn_factory and _FakeHost are hermetic test doubles already used by the existing cross-mode tests. No real CUDA models are loaded.
4. OUTPUT: The _dispatch() method returns {'ok': False, 'error': 'model load failed: ...'} for failed cross-mode toggles. voicectl (via the unchanged format_result()) prints 'error: model load failed: ...' and exits 1. The existing was_listening=False arm-failure path (test_dispatch_toggle_arm_failure_returns_ok_false in test_voicectl.py:368) continues to work unchanged. No new public API.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. This fix makes the behavior match what PRD §4.2bis already documents (arm commands return ok:false on load failure). The README and docs do not describe the toggle failure response, so there is no doc to update inline. The final changeset-level documentation task (P1.M4.T1) will verify no drift.

milestones.title: Issue 2: Reset stale _final_pending on arm/disarm to prevent spurious 5-second drain
milestones.description: The _final_pending flag (set True by _touch_speech on every realtime partial, cleared only by on_final) is never reset in _arm() or _disarm(). After a final lands and on_final clears it, a late/stray partial from the realtime model re-sets it True. This stale True persists across session boundaries and causes _request_stop() to enter the 5-second drain path even when no utterance is in flight, violating the PRD's responsiveness requirement. The fix resets _final_pending=False in _arm() (a fresh arm means no utterance is in flight) and optionally _disarm() for defense in depth.

tasks.title: Reset _final_pending = False in _arm() and _disarm()
tasks.description: Add self._final_pending = False to _arm() (a fresh session has no in-flight utterance) and _disarm() (defense in depth: clear between sessions even without a re-arm). This ensures stale values from late/stray realtime partials do not persist into the next arm/stop cycle.

subtasks.title: Add _final_pending reset to _arm()/_disarm() + stale-partial drain regression test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/006_862ee9d6ef41/bugfix/001_ce96ba984c11/architecture/system_context.md §Issue 2 and _scout_test_patterns.md §Issue 2. The _final_pending flag is: set True by _touch_speech() (daemon.py:1059) on EVERY realtime partial callback; cleared False ONLY by on_final() (daemon.py:973); NEVER reset in _arm() (daemon.py:1006-1019) or _disarm() (daemon.py:1021-1047). _request_stop() (daemon.py:1084) checks `self._host is not None and self._text_in_flight.is_set() and self._final_pending` to decide drain vs immediate-disarm. _DRAIN_TIMEOUT_S = 5.0 (daemon.py:138). The existing test test_stop_aborts_immediately_when_text_idle_no_speech (test_daemon.py:726-734) does NOT call _touch_speech(), so it misses the stale-partial scenario. The drain test block is at test_daemon.py:683-759. Tests use _make_daemon() (test_daemon.py:618-626) returning (d, fb, rec, be) with a _StubRecorder (rec.aborts counts abort calls) and _FakeBackend (be.typed records typed text).
2. INPUT: No upstream subtask dependency. _arm() and _disarm() are standalone methods on VoiceTypingDaemon. The _final_pending attribute is initialized at daemon.py:638 (`self._final_pending: bool = False`). _touch_speech() and on_final() are already correct and need no changes.
3. LOGIC:
   A) TEST FIRST (failing test, RED): Add a test to tests/test_daemon.py in the drain test block (near line 726). The test simulates the stale _final_pending scenario:
      - `d, fb, rec, be = _make_daemon()`
      - `d.start()` → arm (with the fix, _arm() resets _final_pending=False)
      - `d._touch_speech()` → _final_pending=True (speech detected)
      - `d.on_final('hello world')` → _final_pending=False, text typed (the utterance finalized)
      - `d._touch_speech()` → stray late partial → _final_pending=True (STALE — no real speech)
      - Now verify the fix prevents cross-session staleness: `d._complete_drain()` if needed, or simulate stop → then re-arm → assert d._final_pending is False after re-arm.
      - ALSO add a direct cross-session test: `d.start()` → `d._touch_speech()` → `d._disarm()` (via d.stop() which calls _request_stop, or directly under _lock) → assert `d._final_pending is False` after _disarm (the fix in _disarm clears it). Then `d.start()` → assert `d._final_pending is False` after _arm.
      The tests MUST exercise that after a re-arm (or disarm), _final_pending is False regardless of prior stray partials. Before the fix these tests FAIL (RED) because _arm()/_disarm() never reset _final_pending.
      IMPORTANT: The minimal fix (reset on arm/disarm) addresses CROSS-SESSION staleness. The within-session case (stray partial in the SAME session after a final but before stop) is a deeper VAD-vs-partial issue the PRD marks as 'a more thorough fix' — do NOT implement VAD detection; only the _arm()/_disarm() reset.
   B) IMPLEMENT (GREEN): In _arm() (daemon.py:1006-1019), add `self._final_pending = False` right after `self._listening.set()`. In _disarm() (daemon.py:1021-1047), add `self._final_pending = False` near the top (after `self._listening.clear()`). Both are single-line additions. DO NOT modify _touch_speech(), on_final(), _request_stop(), _begin_drain(), _complete_drain(), or _drain_timeout() — they are correct.
   C) VERIFY: Run `timeout 120 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_daemon.py -q -k 'drain or final_pending or stop_aborts or touch_speech'` and `timeout 120 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_daemon.py -q -k 'arm or disarm'`. All must pass including the new tests. The existing drain tests (test_stop_drains_when_utterance_in_flight, test_toggle_off_drains_when_utterance_in_flight) must still pass because they call _touch_speech() WITHIN the armed session (after _arm resets it to False, the explicit _touch_speech() re-sets it True correctly).
   Mocking: No external services — _StubRecorder and _FakeBackend are hermetic test doubles already used by the drain tests.
4. OUTPUT: _arm() and _disarm() each set self._final_pending = False. After a re-arm following a session with stray partials, _final_pending starts False (clean slate). A stop after a stray partial but before any new speech in the fresh session disarms immediately (no spurious drain). No new public API.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. This is an internal lifecycle-state fix. The PRD §4.2 #2 already describes the intended immediate-stop behavior; this fix makes the code match. The final changeset-level documentation task (P1.M4.T1) will verify no drift.

milestones.title: Issue 3: Validate output.backend at config load time
milestones.description: An invalid output.backend value (e.g., a typo like 'wtyp') is currently accepted silently at config load time and only fails later when typing_backends.make_backend() raises ValueError during daemon init, causing a systemd crash-loop. Add __post_init__() validation to OutputConfig mirroring the existing AsrConfig.__post_init__() device validation (VT-005), so the user gets an immediate, actionable ValueError at load time.

tasks.title: Add OutputConfig.__post_init__() with backend value validation
tasks.description: Add a __post_init__() method to the OutputConfig dataclass that validates backend is one of ('wtype', 'ydotool', 'tmux') and raises ValueError otherwise, mirroring the AsrConfig device validation pattern (VT-005). The runtime check in typing_backends.make_backend() stays as a defensive second gate.

subtasks.title: Add backend value validation to OutputConfig.__post_init__() + load-time tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/006_862ee9d6ef41/bugfix/001_ce96ba984c11/architecture/system_context.md §Issue 3 and _scout_test_patterns.md §Issue 3. OutputConfig (config.py:119-125) currently has NO __post_init__. The precedent is AsrConfig.__post_init__ (config.py:72-118) which validates device with `if self.device not in ('cuda', 'cpu'): raise ValueError(...)`. The runtime validation in typing_backends.make_backend() (typing_backends.py:142-164) raises ValueError(f'unknown output.backend: {backend!r}') — this stays as-is (defensive second gate). The valid backend values are confirmed: 'wtype' (_WtypeWithFallback), 'ydotool' (YdotoolBackend), 'tmux' (TmuxBackend). Tests: VT-005 device tests are at test_config.py:172-183 (test_invalid_device_value_raises, test_valid_device_values_load) using `with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='device'): VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml({'asr': {'device': bad}})`. The config must remain stdlib-only (no cuda/torch imports). The README (line ~167) already documents the valid backend values, so no content change is needed there.
2. INPUT: No upstream subtask dependency. OutputConfig is a standalone dataclass. VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml() calls OutputConfig(**section) via _overlay (config.py:248-254), so __post_init__ fires at load time. The make_backend() runtime check is downstream and unchanged.
3. LOGIC:
   A) TEST FIRST (failing test, RED): Add tests to tests/test_config.py near the VT-005 device tests (line ~172). Mirror the VT-005 pattern EXACTLY:
      - test_invalid_backend_value_raises(): `for bad in ('wtyp', 'xterm', 'WTYPE', '', 'auto', 'gpu'): with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='backend'): VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml({'output': {'backend': bad}})` — each must raise ValueError naming 'backend'.
      - test_valid_backend_values_load(): `for good in ('wtype', 'ydotool', 'tmux'): cfg = VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml({'output': {'backend': good}}); assert cfg.output.backend == good` — each must load OK.
      The invalid-value test MUST FAIL (RED) before the fix because OutputConfig has no __post_init__.
   B) IMPLEMENT (GREEN): Add a __post_init__(self) method to OutputConfig (config.py, after the field declarations, before any other method). The body: `if self.backend not in ('wtype', 'ydotool', 'tmux'): raise ValueError(f'[output] backend must be "wtype", "ydotool", or "tmux", got {self.backend!r}')`. Use ValueError (not TypeError) — the TYPE is correct (str), the VALUE is not, matching the device validation precedent. DO NOT modify make_backend() in typing_backends.py — it stays as the defensive runtime gate. DO NOT modify AsrConfig or any other dataclass.
   C) VERIFY: Run `timeout 60 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_config.py -q` and `timeout 60 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_config_repo_default.py -q` and `timeout 60 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_typing_backends.py -q`. All must pass. The repo-default test (test_repo_config_toml_equals_defaults) must still pass because config.toml uses backend='wtype' (a valid value). The existing test_make_backend_unknown_raises_value_error (test_typing_backends.py:236) must still pass (make_backend's own ValueError is unchanged — but note: with __post_init__ validation, OutputConfig(backend='bogus') now raises at CONSTRUCTION, so that test may need adjustment if it constructs OutputConfig directly; verify and adjust if needed — the test should still be valid because pytest.raises catches the ValueError regardless of where it fires).
   Mocking: No external services — config.py is pure stdlib (os, tomllib, dataclasses, pathlib). No imports of cuda/torch/realtimestt.
4. OUTPUT: VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml({'output': {'backend': 'wtyp'}}) raises ValueError at LOAD time (before the daemon starts), giving the user an immediate actionable error. Valid backends load normally. make_backend() retains its own ValueError as a defensive second gate. No new public API.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The README (line ~167) already documents output.backend valid values ('wtype', 'ydotool', 'tmux') — the content is already accurate, so no inline doc update is needed for the valid-values list. However, consider adding a brief note in the AsrConfig.device or output.backend README row that invalid values are rejected at load time (the README already says device is 'cuda or cpu' without mentioning validation, so this is consistent). The final changeset-level documentation task (P1.M4.T1) will verify README consistency.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Final sweep of cross-cutting documentation (README.md feature tables, config tables, any overview docs) to ensure they are consistent with the three fixes. Per SOW §5 Mode B, this task depends on ALL implementing subtasks and runs last. The three fixes are primarily internal correctness/robustness improvements; the README already documents the relevant surfaces (valid backend values, voicectl exit codes), so this is expected to be a verification pass with minor touch-ups at most.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Verify README.md and any overview docs are consistent with the three bug fixes. The changeset adds load-time config validation for output.backend (Issue 3), makes toggle/toggle-lite failures surface as errors (Issue 1), and fixes a stale-state drain bug (Issue 2). The README already documents valid backend values and voicectl behavior, so this is a verification pass.

subtasks.title: Verify and sync README.md + docs for the three-fix changeset
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/006_862ee9d6ef41/bugfix/001_ce96ba984c11/architecture/system_context.md §Documentation Surface. The README.md config table (line ~154-191) already lists output.backend valid values ('wtype', 'ydotool', 'tmux') at line ~167, so the content is accurate for Issue 3. The README does not describe toggle-failure responses or _final_pending drain internals (Issues 1 & 2 are internal correctness fixes with no user-visible behavior change beyond fixing bugs to match documented behavior). There is no separate docs/ directory with CONFIGURATION.md or similar — the README IS the config documentation.
2. INPUT: All three implementing subtasks are complete (P1.M1.T1.S1, P1.M2.T1.S1, P1.M3.T1.S1). The code changes are: _dispatch() toggle/toggle-lite cross-mode failure routing (Issue 1), _arm()/_disarm() _final_pending reset (Issue 2), OutputConfig.__post_init__ backend validation (Issue 3).
3. LOGIC:
   A) Read README.md in full. Verify:
      - The output.backend row (line ~167) is consistent with the new load-time validation. If the README says something like 'auto-falls-back' for wtype, that is still accurate (the fallback is in _WtypeWithFallback, not config). Consider adding a parenthetical '(rejected at load time if invalid)' if it improves clarity, but only if the current text would mislead a user about what happens on a typo.
      - The voicectl exit-code documentation (if any) is consistent with toggle failures now returning exit 1 (the README already documents exit codes 0/1/2/64 per PRD §4.8).
      - No mention of _final_pending, drain internals, or dispatch-layer response shaping needs to be added (these are implementation details not documented in the README).
   B) If the README is already accurate (likely), make no changes and document this finding in the commit message / PR description. If a minor touch-up improves clarity (e.g., noting that config values are validated at load time), apply it.
   C) Verify `timeout 60 .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_config_repo_default.py -q` still passes (the repo-default drift guard checks config.toml == dataclass defaults — the OutputConfig defaults are unchanged).
   Mocking: None — this is a documentation verification task.
4. OUTPUT: README.md (and any other overview docs) are verified consistent with the changeset. Any necessary touch-ups applied. No stale documentation ships with the fixes.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task — it updates/syncs the changeset-level docs (README.md overview, config table). No further doc work is needed after this subtask.
